description:
You are a first-responder forensic analyst. A detective has just received a seized USB drive image and needs a rapid triage of its contents before assigning specialized examiners. Your mission is to categorize all files by MIME type and produce a prioritized triage report.

Create a forensic case named 'USB_Triage_2024' (Case Number: INV-TRG-007) in /home/ga/Cases/ using the disk image /home/ga/evidence/ntfs_undel.dd. Identify the MIME types for the files in the image and generate two reports.

First, write a detailed file inventory to /home/ga/Reports/file_type_inventory.txt. Organize the inventory under exactly these five category headers on their own lines: '=== IMAGES ===', '=== DOCUMENTS ===', '=== EXECUTABLES ===', '=== ARCHIVES ===', and '=== OTHER ==='. Under each header, list files in the format: FILENAME|FULL_PATH|SIZE_BYTES|MIME_TYPE|IS_DELETED (where IS_DELETED is YES or NO). Include at least 60% of the files found in the image.

Second, write a triage summary to /home/ga/Reports/triage_summary.txt containing exactly these lines:
CASE_NAME: USB_Triage_2024
CASE_NUMBER: INV-TRG-007
DATA_SOURCE: ntfs_undel.dd
TOTAL_FILES: <N> (regular files only)
TOTAL_DIRECTORIES: <N>
ALLOCATED_FILES: <N>
DELETED_FILES: <N>
CATEGORY_IMAGES: <N>
CATEGORY_DOCUMENTS: <N>
CATEGORY_EXECUTABLES: <N>
CATEGORY_ARCHIVES: <N>
CATEGORY_OTHER: <N>
LARGEST_FILE: <filename> (<size> bytes)
RECOMMENDATION: <1-2 sentence recommendation based on findings>
